title: ranunculus × digeneus nothosubsp. digeneus
intro: Ranunculus × digeneus nothosubsp. digeneus, also known as the Creeping Buttercup, is a plant species that is a hybrid of Ranunculus repens and Ranunculus bulbosus. It is primarily recognized for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation, due to its content of ranunculin and other alkaloids. The health benefits of Creeping Buttercup include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote wound healing. Creeping Buttercup is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
